title: Check for image with /libpod/containers/create
body:
The libpod containers create endpoint wasn't checking whether the image existed before creating the container. If the image doesn't exist, it should return a 404 status code but it was failing and returning a 500 status code.
This fix matches the behavior of the compat endpoint.

Fixes https://github.com/containers/podman-py/issues/231

<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

Please make sure you've read our contributing guidelines and how to submit a pull request (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#submitting-pull-requests).

In case you're only changing docs, make sure to prefix the pull-request title with "[CI:DOCS]". That will prevent functional tests from running and save time and energy.

Finally, be sure to sign commits with your real name. Since by opening
a PR you already have commits, you can add signatures if needed with
something like `git commit -s --amend`.
-->

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

For more information on release notes, please follow the Kubernetes model:
https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/release-notes.md
-->

```release-note
Return expected 404 status code when image not found with libpod/containers/create endpoint
```

comments.body: It looks like you broke `--rootfs` support in `podman remote`

It doesn't. That bothered me too, so I looked at the test, and, ugh. There's a `Skip()` halfway through, which is a practice that I find objectionable. I've added a TODO for myself to clean that up, but not today.
@edsantiago I moved the `SkipIfRemote` to the beginning of the --rootfs test, so hopefully that should fix the failure

The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)

> @edsantiago I moved the `SkipIfRemote` to the beginning of the --rootfs test, so hopefully that should fix the failure

I don't think that's the long-term correct thing to do, because obviously the test does _something_ under remote, but it's on my TODO list. I'll take care of it another day.
tests failure, I think just test time outs, I didn't restart.

Any idea why the APIV2 tests are not picking up the new changes? When I run the command locally I get the expected 404 return code, but the test is failing because it is still getting the 500 return code.
Ephemeral COPR build failed. @containers/packit-build please check.
